summary: Published tarball ships live npm publish token for @arbocollab scope
details: The published tarball includes `npmjs.npmrc` containing a live npm registry auth token (`//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=npm_...`) scoped to the `@arbocollab` organization. `package.json` declares `"files": ["*"]` and the `.npmignore` does not exclude `npmjs.npmrc`, so the credential is shipped to every installer. Any party who runs `npm install @arbocollab/arbo-web-people` can read the token from their `node_modules` tree and use it to publish arbitrary versions of any `@arbocollab/*` package. This is not author-only self-harm: the credential grants publish rights to a shared scope whose other packages are consumed by downstream installers, so the leaked token directly enables a supply-chain attack against every consumer of the `@arbocollab/*` namespace. The token must be treated as compromised regardless of remediation of this specific version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
